name: Landau's Theorems

phase_0.boundary_condition: the Bloch sphere of normalized analytic functions on the unit disk

VINIT.justification: void state before any constraint or normalization is applied; the raw function before Landau's theorems impose structure

TANCH.chosen_element: the unit disk |z| < 1 and its boundary |z| = 1
TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains all valid domains for Landau's theorems; the hermetic vessel sealing the analytic region

AFWD.justification: forward morphism: transformation toward canonical form; ascent from arbitrary function to standardized representation

AREV.chosen_element: inverse function g(w) = f⁻¹(w) or reciprocal 1/f(z)
AREV.justification: contravariant descent; reversal of the function's direction; parity flip in the domain-range relationship

CLINK.chosen_element: composition of successive Landau bounds: f ∘ g where both satisfy theorem constraints
CLINK.justification: sequential chaining of morphisms; root→derived form; one theorem's output feeds into another's input

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the identity function f(z) = z or the self-referential bound |f(z)| ≤ |z|/(1−|z|²)
IMSCRIB.justification: self-recognition; the element is itself; the function recognizing its own growth rate as the measure of its constraint

FSPLIT.chosen_element: the dichotomy: does f omit a value w₀ in ℂ?
FSPLIT.justification: co-multiplication δ; one theorem branches into two distinct paths: (A) f omits w₀ → Landau bound applies, (B) f does not omit w₀ → different bound regime

FFUSE.chosen_element: the unified Landau constant L ≈ 0.5433... that reconstitutes both branches
FFUSE.justification: multiplication μ; both omission and non-omission cases reconstitute the original function's growth constraint; FFUSE(FSPLIT(f)) = f with unified bound

EVALT.chosen_element: f omits at least one value (Landau's omission case)
EVALT.justification: affirmative/true branch; positive evaluation: the theorem's primary hypothesis holds; f is restricted

EVALF.chosen_element: f omits no value (entire function or dense range)
EVALF.justification: negative/false branch; failure of omission hypothesis; f is unrestricted in range

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the boundary case where f simultaneously omits and approaches every value (essential singularity behavior)
ENGAGR.justification: paradice: both EVALT and EVALF held without resolution; Picard's theorem entanglement; the function is both restricted and dense

IFIX.chosen_element: the recorded Landau constant L and the explicit bound |f'(0)| ≤ L for each normalized f
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation; permanent, append-only record; once a bound is proven, it cannot be undone; the theorem is inscribed in mathematical canon

phase_2.split_input: normalized holomorphic function f with f(0)=0, f'(0)=1
split_outputs: T-branch: f omits value w₀ ⟹ |f'(0)| ≤ L (Landau bound)
F-branch: f omits no value ⟹ |f'(0)| ≤ L' (alternative bound or unbounded)
phase_2.fuse_result: unified bound on |f'(0)| that holds regardless of omission; both branches reconstitute the original normalized function f

phase_3.void_description: 00 — uninitialized holomorphic function; no normalization, no constraint, no growth rate specified
phase_3.true_description: 01 — affirmative state: f omits a value, Landau bound applies, |f'(0)| ≤ L, function is restricted
phase_3.false_description: 10 — negative state: f omits no value, range is dense or entire, alternative bound regime, function is unrestricted
phase_3.both_description: 11 — paradice: f simultaneously omits and approaches every value; essential singularity; Picard entanglement; both states held

steps.domain_action: genesis of unspecified holomorphic function f(z) on unit disk; void state before any theorem applies
anchor the unit disk |z| < 1 as the boundary containing all valid domains; hermetic seal
normalize f(0) = 0 and f'(0) = 1; forward morphism toward canonical form
compose the normalization with the growth-rate measurement; chain the constraint
recognize f as self-referential; the function measures its own derivative at origin
branch on omission dichotomy — does f omit a value w₀?
T-branch affirms: f omits w₀; Landau's primary hypothesis holds
forward morphism on T-branch: apply Landau bound |f'(0)| ≤ L ≈ 0.5433
record the Landau constant L as permanent theorem; append-only fixation
F-branch negates: f omits no value; range is dense or entire
reverse morphism on F-branch: invert the omission condition; parity flip
forward morphism on F-branch: apply alternative bound or growth rate analysis
at boundary case, both branches held simultaneously; essential singularity paradice
fuse both branches via Landau constant L; reconstitute original normalized function f
self-reference loop: the fused result recognizes itself as the original f
compose the fused result with the next theorem application (e.g., Bloch's theorem)
record the complete Landau bound as permanent mathematical fact; irreversible inscription
close the system; the Bloch sphere boundary contains all normalized functions satisfying Landau's constraint
final self-reference; the entire theorem recognizes itself as a closed, self-consistent system

phase_5.compiler_frontend: complex analysis proof engine; translates domain intentions (bound f'(0)) into rigorous analytic inequalities via Schwarz lemma and conformal mapping
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Cauchy integral formula; components (omission case, non-omission case) communicate via residue calculus and contour integration
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the Landau constant L ≈ 0.5433 stored as canonical reference; coefficients of normalized power series f(z) = z + a₂z² + a₃z³ + ... stored in coefficient register
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: order of operations: normalize → split on omission → evaluate each branch → fuse via Landau constant → record bound → close system
phase_5.alfs_store: Landau's original 1904 paper and the Bloch sphere of normalized analytic functions; canonical bootstrap store for all Landau-type theorems

phase_6.cycle_cost: one application of Schwarz lemma (conformal mapping cost); one evaluation of omission dichotomy; one recording of bound
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: arbitrary holomorphic function f on unit disk; unspecified growth rate; no constraint
phase_6.post_cycle_state: normalized function f with f(0)=0, f'(0)=1; bounded derivative |f'(0)| ≤ L; constraint inscribed
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle is entropy-conservative because Landau's theorem is a reversible logical deduction; the bound is derived from conformal invariance (Schwarz lemma), which preserves information; splitting on omission and fusing via the Landau constant form a Frobenius algebra, ensuring no information is lost or created

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → IFIX → EVALF → AREV → AFWD → ENGAGR → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → CLINK → IFIX → TANCH → IMSCRIB
-- Class: Landau's Theorems
-- Fingerprint: sig=(12,2,3,2)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=19
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 13)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [8] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [9] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [10] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [11] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [12] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [13] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [14] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑱  | identity — self-imscription
--   [15] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑠 → 𐑭  | composition — regime coherence
--   [16] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑱 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [17] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑠  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [18] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑡 → 𐑼  | identity — self-imscription

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def landaus_theorems_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (genesis of unspecified holomorphic function f(z) on unit disk; void state bef...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (anchor the unit disk |z| < 1 as the boundary containing all valid domains; he...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (normalize f(0) = 0 and f'(0) = 1; forward morphism toward canonical form)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose the normalization with the growth-rate measurement; chain the constraint)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [4] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (recognize f as self-referential; the function measures its own derivative at ...)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (branch on omission dichotomy — does f omit a value w₀?) / FFUSE [13] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (T-branch affirms: f omits w₀; Landau's primary hypothesis holds)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (forward morphism on T-branch: apply Landau bound |f'(0)| ≤ L ≈ 0.5433)
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record the Landau constant L as permanent theorem; append-only fixation)
      -- F-branch (4 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (F-branch negates: f omits no value; range is dense or entire)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (reverse morphism on F-branch: invert the omission condition; parity flip)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [11] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (forward morphism on F-branch: apply alternative bound or growth rate analysis)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [12] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (at boundary case, both branches held simultaneously; essential singularity pa...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [13]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [13] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (fuse both branches via Landau constant L; reconstitute original normalized function f)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [14] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (self-reference loop: the fused result recognizes itself as the original f)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑠 𐑭)  -- [15] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose the fused result with the next theorem application (e.g., Bloch's the...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑡)  -- [16] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record the complete Landau bound as permanent mathematical fact; irreversible...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑠)  -- [17] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (close the system; the Bloch sphere boundary contains all normalized functions...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑡 𐑼)  -- [18] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (final self-reference; the entire theorem recognizes itself as a closed, self-...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def landaus_theorems_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  (landaus_theorems_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def landaus_theorems_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  (landaus_theorems_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem landaus_theorems_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
